_methodology: Ground truth from reading each REAL published, built/minified extension (fetched from pinned GitHub releases). Same labeling rules as corpus/labels.json. Minified code preserves chrome.* member names, so namespace usage stays grep-able; sensitive Tab reads and webpack-module-wrapped API access (e.g. a minified webextension-polyfill alias) are the hard cases.

_excluded.violentmonkey: Robustness-only (in release-sources.json, parses without crashing). Excluded from accuracy: it accesses the API via `T.contextMenus` where T is a webpack-minified webextension-polyfill module import — static analysis can't resolve the module, so PERM001 false-positives on contextMenus. A known deep-bundling limitation; precisely why PERM001 ships at `fix`, not `reject`.

extensions.note: MV3. alarms + scripting + storage all used (grep of minified bundle); fontSettings unmapped. host *://*/* -> broad (PERM005).
MV3. storage + scripting used; unlimitedStorage unmapped. Host is specific (youtube / sponsor.ajay.app) -> not broad.

MV3. alarms/contextMenus/storage/webRequest/identity/scripting all used; DNR used in code (declarativeNetRequestWithHostAccess grants it); tabs redundant with <all_urls> (PERM004) + broad host (PERM005). new Function in bundled LESS/Stylus compilers -> MV3002 (fix in v0.1, may be sandboxed/unreachable). No reject -> needs_fixes.
MV3. storage used. web_accessible_resources reference ryd.script.js / menu-fixer.js which are absent from the package (FUNC001 reject); new Function JSON-parse fallback in bundle (MV3002).
MV2 -> MV3003 reject. tabs + broad content-script match -> PERM004/PERM005. No genuinely-unused mapped permission.
MV2 -> MV3003 reject. Uses the brws = globalThis.browser || globalThis.chrome polyfill idiom: alarms/storage/webRequest all resolved as used after the logical-alias fix.
